steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Identify the hypotenuse
steps.content: Since angle $Q$ is $90^\circ$, $PR$ is the hypotenuse. The problem gives $PQ=20$ and says $PR$ is $9$ greater than $PQ$, so $PR=29$.

steps.title: Write the same area two ways
steps.content:
The altitude $QS$ goes from the right angle to the hypotenuse. The area of triangle $PQR$ can be written using either pair of base and height:
  • Using the legs: $\frac{1}{2}\cdot PQ\cdot QR$
  • Using the hypotenuse and altitude: $\frac{1}{2}\cdot PR\cdot QS$
Set these equal and cancel $\frac{1}{2}$: $$PQ\cdot QR=PR\cdot QS$$

steps.title: Solve for the requested ratio
steps.content: Divide both sides by $PQ\cdot QS$: $$\frac{QR}{QS}=\frac{PR}{PQ}=\frac{29}{20}$$ This matches the correct answer.
